title: Stokes's Theorem Implies Net Zero Charge
summary: Because the relevant form is closed on the punctured Brillouin zone, integrating its exterior derivative over the punctured region and applying Stokes's theorem yields that the sum of boundary charges around all bad points is zero.
assumptions: Bad points are isolated and excised to form the punctured region.
regime: Global topological analysis on the punctured Brillouin zone.
scope: Lecture One degree-theoretic and Berry-curvature proofs of chirality cancellation.

source_anchors.notes: The theorem is derived from a closed form on the punctured Brillouin zone.
The Berry-curvature proof has the same Stokes structure.

representation: Global derivation using a closed form and boundary spheres around bad points.

math_expressions.notes: Degree-theory version of the charge-cancellation result.
Berry-curvature version of the same cancellation law.

failure_modes: The simple derivation must be reformulated for non-isolated node sets.
retrieval_hints: Use when a query asks why local node charges must add to zero globally.
